Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PHILIP RASKIN's Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Philip Raskin's motion for leave to file a third amended complaint adding Curt Welker as an additional plaintiff is granted on the following condition, to which plaintiff must agree either orally at the hearing or in a writing sent to counsel for defendants prior to the hearing. The condition is that the trial is continued to November 2, 2020, with all discovery deadlines triggered off the new trial date. If plaintiff does not agree to this condition, the motion is denied. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. (See, e.g., Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) The condition on the granting of the motion eliminates any legal prejudice to defendants by the granting of the motion, including their inability prior to the existing trial date to file a timely motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication as to the claims to be newly asserted by Mr. Welker. If defendants wish to challenge those claims, they may do so by way of demurrer to the proposed amended complaint. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the complaint 'and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.'"].)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
